message:
test(metamorphic): add four inode-checksum sensitivity / detection relations (bd-z75f6)

The harness in crates/ffs-core/tests/metamorphic.rs previously covered a
single relation: that restamping an inode ignores any prior bytes in the
checksum field.  That property says the stamping function is well-defined
but says nothing about whether the stamped digest actually catches the
attacks ext4's checksum protocol is meant to catch.

This adds four new metamorphic proptests that pin the digest's behavioural
contract against the corresponding attack class:

- MR-INO (`inode_checksum_is_sensitive_to_inode_number`): identical inode
  bodies stamped at two different inode numbers must yield different
  on-disk checksums, and a stamp from one inode must not verify when
  presented as another.  Catches inode-block replay across slots.

- MR-SEED (`inode_checksum_is_sensitive_to_csum_seed`): stamping the same
  body+ino under two different filesystem checksum seeds must yield
  different checksums, and a stamp from seed A must not verify under
  seed B.  Catches cross-filesystem stamp replay.

- MR-GEN (`inode_checksum_is_sensitive_to_generation`): flipping only the
  generation field must shift the stored checksum.  Confirms that
  generation actually participates in the per-inode seed (the standard
  ext4 NFS-handle anti-replay mechanism).

- MR-DETECT (`inode_checksum_detects_single_bit_flip_in_body`): flipping
  any single bit in any byte outside the four checksum-field bytes must
  invalidate verification.  Confirms the digest covers the full inode
  body — no truncation, no holes.

Also adds two helpers reused across the cases: `extract_stamped_checksum`
(reads the lo/hi halves into a u32) and `is_checksum_byte` (so MR-DETECT
can `prop_assume!` away the four bytes whose flip is invisible by
construction).

All five relations now run as part of `cargo test -p ffs-core --test
metamorphic` (verified via rch); clippy --tests -- -D warnings clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
